Tests object creation and property assignment in a JavaScript function.

# Purpose
This code defines a simple function [`f`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-703f>) that takes two parameters, `a` and `b`, and returns an object with properties `a` and `b` set to the values of the corresponding parameters. The function is then called with the arguments `'1'` and `'2'`, and the result is stored in the variable `o`. The code includes two assertions to verify that the properties `a` and `b` of the object `o` are equal to `'1'` and `'2'`, respectively. This script provides narrow functionality, primarily serving as a basic demonstration of object creation and property assignment in JavaScript.

### f<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-703.f}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry/regression-test-issue-703.js#L16>)

Creates and returns an object with properties `a` and `b` set to the input arguments.
- **Inputs**:
    - `a`: The value to assign to the `a` property of the returned object.
    - `b`: The value to assign to the `b` property of the returned object.
- **Control Flow**:
    - Return an object with properties `a` and `b` set to the values of the input arguments `a` and `b`.
- **Output**: An object with properties `a` and `b` corresponding to the input arguments.
